mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment typically correlates with economic growth.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate profits decline over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over the last 3 months.
evidence_refs: fact:Rising unemployment often precedes or accompanies recessions.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales are robust, but the federal funds rate is kept low, it indicates strong consumer demand supported by accommodative monetary policy, which is highly favorable for equity market performance over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Low interest rates typically stimulate consumer spending.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with strong demand
failure_condition: If the trigger condition is met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment, stable consumer prices allow for sustained economic growth without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, supporting corporate valuations and S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Low and stable inflation is generally positive for equity markets.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL 12-month percentage change is consistently below 2.5%, but S&P 500 60-day returns show no statistically significant positive bias.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts indicates growing confidence in the economy and future demand, stimulating related industries and contributing to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average rises by more than 5%.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading indicator of economic activity.
failure_condition: If HOUST 3-month moving average rises by more than 5%, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth signals increasing manufacturing output and capacity utilization, reflecting robust economic activity and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production reflects the health of the manufacturing sector.
failure_condition: If INDPRO 1-month percentage change is consistently above 0.3%, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate indicates an easing monetary policy stance, which reduces borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, thereby boosting equity valuations and S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls by more than 0.1 percentage points over the last 3 months.
evidence_refs: fact:Lower interest rates generally support higher equity prices.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS falls by more than 0.1pp over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth coupled with a declining unemployment rate signifies a healthy and expanding economy, which provides a strong fundamental backdrop for corporate earnings and S&P 500 appreciation over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 1-quarter percentage change (annualized) > 2.0% AND UNRATE falls by more than 0.2 percentage points over the last 3 months.
evidence_refs: fact:Economic growth and falling unemployment are key drivers of market performance.
failure_condition: If the trigger condition is met, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods over a 5-year rolling window.
